Off Calendar Dec-21-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Zoom Conference
J. David Bournazian, Esq.., K&L GATES LLP, 949-253-0900 david.Bournazian@klgates.com Attorney for Plaintiff SKYLINE CAPITAL BUILDERS LLC
DIONE GARLICK, ESQ., GIBSON, DUNN & CRUTCHER LLP 213.229.7000 dgarlick@gibsondunn.com Attorneys for Defendants CROSSHARBOR CAPITAL PARTNERS LLC CH SDF LENDER LLC "CROSSHARBOR SDF LENDER LLC"
BRIAN GEE, Esq., GREENBERG TRAURIG, LLP (415) 655-1300 geeb@gtlaw.com Attorneys for Defendant
SFII SHARON, LLC

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF SKYLINE CAPITAL BUILDERS LLC, MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-23-607161, CGC-23-607203, CGC-23-607206, CGC-23-607223, CGC-23-607439, CGC-23-607791, CGC-23-607823, CGC-23-608019, CGC-23-609034 WITH CASE CGC-23-606211

Plaintiff's motion to deem cases CGC-23-606211, CGC-23-607161, CGC-23-607203, CGC-23-607206, CGC-23-607223, CGC-23-607439, CGC-23-607791, CGC-23-607823, CGC-23-608019, and CGC-23-609034 related is granted. (CRC 3.300(h).) The parties may seek a single assignment from the Presiding Judge in department 206. The motion to consolidate is denied. While there is overlap among the cases, the subcontractor actions have simple and discrete issues. Consolidation will cause prejudice, delay, and create manageability issues. The parties are ordered to coordinate discovery to reduce duplication of effort.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File And Serve The Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Nov-13-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:

J. David Bournazian, Esq., in court, of K&L GATES LLP, (949) 253-0900, for Plaintiff.

BRIAN GEE, via Zoom, of GREENBERG TRAURIG, LLP, (415) 655-1300, for Defendant SFII SHARON, LLC.

DIONE GARLICK, Esq., via Zoom, of GIBSON, DUNN & CRUTCHER LLP, (213) 229-7000, for Defendants CROSSHARBOR CAPITAL PARTNERS LLC CH SDF LENDER LLC "CROSSHARBOR SDF LENDER LLC".

RE: Plaintiff's Motion For Leave To File And Serve The Second Amended Complaint.
Ruling is argued. At conclusion of argument, the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff's motion for leave to file a second amended complaint is granted. "That trial courts are to liberally permit such amendments, at any stage of the proceeding, has been established policy in this state since 1901." (Hirsa v. Sup. Ct. (1981) 118 Cal.App.3d 486, 488-89; see also CCP Sec. 428.50(c).) A proposed amended complaint's validity is not considered when deciding leave to amend. (See Kittredge Sports Co. v. Sup. Ct. (1989) 213 Cal.App.3d 1045, 1048.)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer. Clerk: Robert Woods. Court Reporter: Judi Johnson, T: 516.965.6565, E: officialreporter@pm.me. (302/RBU)
